changes.details: LOA Tracker can now require Discord login before the app opens. Approved users are checked against the configured whitelist JSON, including secret Gist-backed whitelists with Discord IDs and optional display names.

changes.description: Discord auth uses Authorization Code with PKCE.
changes.details: The login flow opens Discord in the user's browser, uses a local callback listener for the authorization code, and exchanges it with PKCE so no Discord client secret is shipped in the desktop app.

changes.description: Approved Discord users are remembered locally.
changes.details: After approval, the app stores the user's Discord ID in meowgang_auth.txt under the LOA Tracker app data folder. On startup, that stored ID is rechecked against the current whitelist so users do not need to log in every time.

changes.description: A Discord welcome screen was added.
changes.details: Approved users now see a small welcome screen with their whitelist display name and a Proceed button before the main app loads.

changes.description: Welcome screen visibility can be configured.
changes.details: System Settings now includes a Show Welcome Screen toggle. Users who disable it will go directly into the app after their stored Discord access is approved.
The login screen now uses the LOA Tracker icon and title, shows a clearer MeowGang-only access message, and uses the correct login wording when no stored Discord ID exists.

changes.description: Denied Discord users get a clearer response.
changes.details: Stored IDs that are not approved now show the Not approved by our Meowtator message with a Tenor animation and a local fallback animation if the embed cannot load.

changes.description: The approved username now lives in the sidebar.
changes.details: The Welcome username badge was moved out of the header and into the bottom of the sidebar, with smaller centered text so it does not crowd the reset timer or setup guide button.

changes.description: Discord login opens in the browser correctly on Windows.
changes.details: The browser launch path was adjusted so OAuth URLs are opened through the default browser instead of being interpreted by PowerShell or File Explorer.

changes.description: Empty local auth files now return to login.
changes.details: If meowgang_auth.txt is missing or empty, the app shows Log in with Discord instead of treating the user as a denied account.

changes.description: Whitelist display names are used consistently.
changes.details: When a whitelist entry contains a name, the app now uses that name for welcome messaging instead of falling back to the raw Discord ID.

changes.description: README privacy notes were updated for Discord auth.
changes.details: The documentation now explains that local app data stays on the machine while Discord login contacts Discord and the configured whitelist source for access verification.
Development setup now documents the Discord client ID, callback URL, whitelist URL override, supported whitelist formats, and the local auth cache file location.
